title: AUTONOMOUS AEROBOT: THERMAL CONTROL SUBSYSTEM DESIGN
what: Thermal subsystem lead for a fully autonomous Venus aerobot mission concept targeting 370 K thermal equilibrium across a 350–400 K atmospheric profile at 35–50 km altitude

Two-tier passive thermal control architecture: SilcoNert® 1040 low-emissivity coating (ε = 0.008, α = 0.008, T_max = 450 K, TRL 5, 1 m²) + ACT variable-view-factor radiator (4.0 kg, 0.8×0.3×0.3 m, TRL 5) — 0 W, no sensors or control logic
Mission requirements defined and verified through NASA systems engineering lifecycle (NPR 7123.1) from MCR through PDR
how: Churchill-Bernstein Nu applied to Venus CO₂ flow (Re = 6.43×10⁴, ρ = 1.96 kg/m³, Pr = 1.13, μ = 27.5×10⁻⁶ Pa·s) → Q_conv = 29.9 kW = 97.8% of 30.6 kW hot-case load
Trade matrix (reliability/performance 40% each, cost/mass 10%): SilcoNert® 1040 → 100% vs Dunmore 82%; ACT radiator 84% — 0 W passive selected over Tempco SHK (92%)
Verification: Venus-analog chamber, 350–400 K cycling; acceptance ±5 K at 370 K; TH-2.1 (equilibrium), TH-2.3 (ε ≤ 0.01), TH-2.4 (≥30/11 kW) verified at PDR
results: ≥30 kW cooling (400 K hot case) + ≥11 kW heating (350 K cold case) at 0 W — ACT vapor-pressure self-regulation, no sensors or control system
50× radiative reduction: 466.64 → 9.33 W; 200× solar attenuation: 65 → 0.325 W — both via SilcoNert® ε = 0.008
TH-2.1–2.4 verified at PDR; 4.0 kg, 0 W — mass and power within mission allocation ($6.3M, 4 kg constraint)
